pattern: Since only the letter multiset matters for an anagram, the answer is the total surplus of letters in one string relative to the other — a frequency difference summed over the alphabet.
hints: Replacing a character only changes counts, so reorderings are irrelevant; compare how many of each letter each string has.
Wherever the target string has fewer of some letter than it needs, those slots must each be rewritten — that shortfall is what you pay.
Tally letter frequencies for both strings. For every letter, add max(0, count_in_s - count_in_t); that sum is the minimum number of replacements.

edge_inputs.description: completely disjoint alphabets, all must change

stress.description: two long random lowercase strings of equal length over a small alphabet
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 50000))
    import string
    alpha = string.ascii_lowercase[:6]
    s = ''.join(rng.choice(alpha) for _ in range(n))
    t = ''.join(rng.choice(alpha) for _ in range(n))
    return [s, t]
stress.note: equal length is required by the problem; small alphabet forces overlaps
